cot:
The opponent opens with the highly aggressive Incineroar + Urshifu-RS lead. My Calyrex-Shadow is threatened by a potential OHKO from Incineroar's Knock Off (if 4x weak) or a Scarf Surging Strikes from Urshifu-RS, which would outspeed a non-Scarf Calyrex. Smeargle is prime Fake Out bait. 

To seize immediate momentum and mitigate these worst-case scenarios, I'll Terastallize Calyrex into Ghost. This eliminates the 4x Dark weakness, allowing it to survive a Knock Off from Incineroar (an Adamant 0+ Atk Knock Off deals 93-110% to Tera Ghost, meaning it likely survives, especially if Focus Sash or if Incineroar is bulky). Scarf Urshifu's Surging Strikes caps at 92.5% against Calyrex-Shadow, so Tera Ghost guarantees we live its assault. In return, a Tera Ghost Astral Barrage will comfortably OHKO standard Urshifu-RS (even with just Focus Sash or Life Orb) and punch a solid chunk into Incineroar.

Simultaneously, I'll pivot Smeargle out into Tornadus. Smeargle is too valuable to lose or get its Sash broken for free on turn 1. Tornadus absorbs the likely Fake Out directed at that slot and positions us perfectly to set up Prankster Tailwind next turn, cementing our speed advantage while preserving Smeargle for late-game disruption or redirection.

cot: Miraidon is at 36% HP, meaning any attack from Zamazenta or Incineroar will knock it out, wasting its remaining utility. Switching to Ogerpon-Hearthflame completely preserves Miraidon while positioning Ogerpon (which resists nothing here but easily tanks Body Press, taking 70-83%) to handle the incoming attacks. Crucially, Incineroar is still on the field from Turn 1 (its Parting Shot failed into Terapagos's Protect), so it cannot use Fake Out this turn. This guarantees Terapagos will not flinch. Terapagos is at 100% HP, so Tera Shell is active. This makes all incoming damage 'not very effective' (0.5x). At +1 Def (from Electric Seed), Zamazenta's Body Press does drastically reduced damage to Terapagos, while Ogerpon surviving the other slot's attack is highly likely since Incineroar used Parting Shot last turn and typically cycles it to pivot. Terapagos gets a free Calm Mind, snowballing its SpA and SpD safely behind Tera Shell, setting up my win condition early while mitigating the worst-case scenario.

  Item: Focus Sash 100% | Ability: Moody 94% | Tera: Grass 52% (Ghost 46%)
cot:
The opponent has brought in Prankster Sableye after a classic end-of-turn Moody Eject Pack from Smeargle. Sableye's most threatening move here is Encore, which would severely punish Terapagos for protecting last turn by locking it into Protect (which would likely fail and leave it vulnerable). Flutter Mane is at a trivial 4% HP and also a prime target for disruption or simply dying to any chip. 

Kyogre is guaranteed to fire off another rain-boosted spread move (Origin Pulse). If I leave Terapagos in, it risks being entirely neutralized by Encore while taking heavy damage. To clear the Encore threat, I need to switch Terapagos out. Ogerpon-Cornerstone is the perfect switch-in here, as its Sturdy ability ensures it will survive any single attack from Kyogre, maintaining its presence for the following turn. 

At the same time, hard-switching Flutter Mane to Calyrex-Shadow is highly counter-intuitive since Calyrex-Shadow would normally be heavily threatened by Kyogre. However, if I were to sacrifice Flutter Mane, Sableye might choose to burn its turn Encoring or disabling the wrong slot, or Kyogre could theoretically click Protect on a hard read (though less likely if it's Choice Scarf locked into Origin Pulse). The primary advantage of a double switch here is resetting all board conditions: Terapagos dodges the Encore, Ogerpon guarantees survival with Sturdy, and Calyrex-Shadow comes in. While risking Calyrex-Shadow into Origin Pulse is dangerous, Kyogre's Origin Pulse without Mystic Water (if Scarfed to enable this exact Moody Smeargle lead) or with a more defensive spread has a high chance to leave Calyrex alive, positioning me with my two strongest offensive pieces on the field while preserving my protected win-condition Terapagos in the back. Given the constraints and the extreme punishment of an Encore on Terapagos, the double switch is the only line that guarantees keeping the game state completely in my hands without conceding Terapagos's utility.

[Rillaboom]
  Spread 1 (11%): Jolly  140 HP / 84 Atk / 4 Def / 28 SpD / 252 Spe
  Spread 2 (8%): Adamant  252 HP / 36 Atk / 76 Def / 124 SpD / 20 Spe
  Moves (top): Fake Out 100% · Grassy Glide 99% · U-turn 98% · Wood Hammer 97%
  Item: Assault Vest 96% | Ability: Grassy Surge 100% | Tera: Water 57% (Fire 42%)
cot:
With my Terapagos fully set up (+1 SpA, +2 SpD, +1 Def, Tera Stellar) and currently at 100% HP, the opponent's only real answers are physical pressure from Ogerpon-Hearthflame and cycle disruption from Incineroar. Incineroar is sitting at 45% HP after taking a Tera Starstorm last turn. 

Looking at the field, Ogerpon-H can try to use Follow Me to protect Incineroar or hit Chien-Pao/Terapagos. However, Tera Starstorm is a spread move, so it completely ignores redirection. Given the damage Tera Starstorm did last turn (55%), it is mathematically guaranteed to KO Incineroar from 45% even if it is a bulky 252 HP / 60 SpD variant (minimum damage is 95 HP, while 45% is ~91 HP). 

By targeting Incineroar with Ice Spinner, I accomplish two things:
1. If Incineroar stays and Ogerpon doesn't redirect, Ice Spinner ensures the KO on Incineroar even if my Tera Starstorm low-rolls unexpectedly.
2. If Incineroar switches to Rillaboom, Ice Spinner hits the incoming Rillaboom for massive super-effective damage, potentially picking up a KO alongside Tera Starstorm.
3. If Ogerpon uses Follow Me, Ice Spinner gets redirected and chips Ogerpon (around 40-47%), while Tera Starstorm bypasses redirection and KOs Incineroar anyway.

Chien-Pao is at full HP, so it survives an unboosted Ivy Cudgel if Ogerpon attacks it directly, while Terapagos is practically unkillable at +1 Def. This line guarantees I remove Incineroar or severely punish a Rillaboom switch-in, securing an insurmountable endgame advantage.
